description: Higher maximum token entropy catches one uncertain token inside a span.
Higher mean token entropy indicates more uncertainty.
Higher mean token negative log probability indicates more uncertainty.
Higher value means lower average top-2 margin across the span.
Higher value means at least one token has a low top-2 margin.
Maximum same-step selected-token energy gap; equivalent to a max token NLL control.
Higher maximum adjacent-step energy mismatch catches one inconsistent token inside a span.
Higher mean adjacent-step energy mismatch indicates more inconsistency.
Signed Spilled Energy delta averaged over the span.
Higher non-top1 probability mass indicates a less concentrated token distribution.
Higher probability mass outside the top two choices indicates diffuse token uncertainty.
Opposite direction of signed Spilled Energy delta for direction-sensitivity checks.
